methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $22M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Space & Aerospace (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 25 same-sector peers exist in Space & Aerospace, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Space & Aerospace P/S median is 42.9x across 6 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
Magdrive is a Seed-stage space and aerospace company founded by Mark Stokes and Thomas Clayson that has raised $22.5M to date. The valuation landscape includes five public comparables (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and a broader private peer set anchored by SpaceX, Rocket Lab, Blue Origin, Sierra Space, and Planet Labs.

Of the three valuation methods considered, only stage-progression proved applicable. Using total raised as a proxy base value of $22M and applying a typical 2.5x multiplier for progression from Seed to the next stage yields an implied valuation of $56M. Peer-median analysis was not viable due to insufficient narrow peer datapoints within the same stage and valuation tier in Space & Aerospace (only one adjacent-stage comparable identified against a minimum threshold of three). Public-comp anchoring via P/S multiple could not be executed because the target's revenue is not on file; the sector median P/S across the six public comparables stands at 42.9x, but this approach requires disclosed revenue to be meaningful for a private company at this stage.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $48M to $65M, centered at $56M. This range reflects limited triangulation depth—reliance on a single method introduces material uncertainty. Key risks to valuation include revenue trajectory assumptions embedded in the stage-progression model and the absence of directly comparable Seed-stage Space & Aerospace transactions to ground the 2.5x multiplier.
